design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot the ST Falcon tank as gunner in one-on-one duels; charge energy bolts, intercept enemy projectiles, target enemy weak points, manage limited resources across multiple encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time targeting accuracy and timing versus enemy attack prediction; resource management (charged bolts) versus immediate defense needs
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Operate a powerful military tank as precision gunner, engaging in strategic one-on-one mecha combat with varied enemy tactics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Light-gun peripheral requirement forces direct aiming interaction; emphasis on weak-point targeting and defensive interception creates skill-based asymmetry; each enemy ST has distinct attack strategies requiring adaptive tactics rather than pattern memorization alone

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Training mode provides seven tutorial lessons for skill development; multiple game modes (Battle, Time Trial, Combat) allow players to practice; unlocking ST Tornado after campaign completion offers alternative defensive playstyle for replayability

progression.upgrade_system: Vehicle upgrade: ST Tornado unlocked after defeating single-player campaign; ST Tornado trades offensive capacity (stores up to 10 individual energy beams) for reduced defense compared to ST Falcon
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story progression through Battle mode: defeat Battle Game chiefs, then Anubis, then Eltorian alien invaders to unlock ST Tornado
replayability_hooks: Time Trial mode encourages speedrun optimization
Combat versus mode for competitive play with asymmetric loadouts
ST Tornado playstyle offers mechanically distinct experience with weapon storage mechanic

world_building_techniques: Plot exposition: three-year gap after Battle Clash predecessor; Anubis returned for renewed tyranny
Faction introduction: Eltorian alien race as new threat alongside returning antagonist
Character presence: Mike Anderson as ST Falcon pilot, Carol Eugene as ST Tornado pilot, Rola as training instructor
Thematic progression: triumph to betrayal cycle (prosperity undermined by Anubis return)

boss_design.philosophy: Each enemy ST exhibits distinct strategic profile requiring adaptive tactics; emphasis on weak-point vulnerability and projectile interception creates learning curve progression
notable_bosses: ST Cobra (encountered in Battle Game phase)
Eltorian alien race STs (final campaign tier)
Multiple Battle Game chiefs (early campaign encounters with varied attack strategies)

faction_indicators: Battle Game: organized military tournament structure with ranked chiefs
Anubis Empire: authoritarian regime seeking to reclaim power
Eltorian Invaders: external alien threat to planetary sovereignty
danger_signals: Return of defeated tyrant (Anubis) signals institutional instability
Alien invasion represents existential threat requiring unified defense
Multiple simultaneous threats (internal insurgency + external invasion) create civilization-scale crisis
environmental_storytelling: Standing Tank (ST) technology as symbol of military advancement and power balance
Three-year peace period between games establishes false stability
Tank variants (ST Falcon versus ST Tornado) reflect different strategic philosophies in conflict response
